meta.title: Dr. Jorge Arce — Oral & Maxillofacial Surgery · v19 Cunliffe
meta.description: Five chapters, two countries, one discipline. The story of Dr. Jorge Arce, board-certified oral & maxillofacial surgeon in the South Bay.

sections.statement: Oral & maxillofacial surgeon taking the cases <g>other surgeons refer out.</g> <h>Board certified in implantology.</h> Patients keep their lives. <g>He keeps the craft.</g>
sections.note: “Above the fold” was never the point. The work is below — twenty-seven years of it. Scroll for the story.

sections.quote: I never fit the mold of a quick-fix surgeon. Difficult work, done with care, gives people their lives back.

sections.title: The man behind <g>the work.</g>
paras: Jorge grew up in Colombia, the son of a family that valued precision and patience long before he ever held a scalpel. Medicine was not a career choice so much as a calling.
He moved to California to specialize and never left. Over twenty-seven years he built a practice on a single, unglamorous principle: take the time the case actually needs.

sections.title: Story<g>.</g>
sections.sub: Five chapters. Two countries. One discipline, refined for nearly three decades.

items.title: Precision was the <g>family trade.</g>
items.body: Before the operating room there was a household that treated careful work as a form of respect. Jorge trained as a dentist in Colombia and found the discipline that would hold his attention for a lifetime: surgery of the mouth, face, and jaw.
items.serif: Medicine was a way to use his hands to give something back.

items.title: He came to specialize. <g>He never left.</g>
items.body: California offered the depth he was looking for: advanced implantology, complex extractions, reconstruction. He devoted his career entirely to oral and maxillofacial surgery — and to the cases most clinicians prefer to send elsewhere.
items.serif: Board certified in implantology, he made the difficult cases his ordinary day.

items.title: A practice built on <g>one conviction.</g>
items.body: In 2007 he founded the Dental Implants Center of South Bay — Redondo Beach and Torrance — guided by the belief that difficult work, done with care, gives people their lives back.
items.serif: Not a volume clinic. A referral destination for the cases that need a surgeon willing to slow down.

items.title: The work others <g>step away from.</g>
items.body: Dental implants through full-arch All-On-X. Reconstruction where bone and tissue have been lost. Complex extractions, implant maintenance, pathology, and second opinions for patients facing hard decisions.
items.serif: Not a list of services — the disciplines refined over nearly three decades.

items.title: Still reading. Still refining. <g>Still here.</g>
items.body: Twenty-seven years in, he is a father, a mentor to younger surgeons, and a quiet student of his own craft — treating every patient as the person they are before the procedure they need.

cells.l: Founder, Dental Implants Center of South Bay

sections.title: Have a case? <g>Let's talk.</g>
